signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 114.01 — NZD dominates G8 performance as the 1W leader while JPY remains the laggard. Despite NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading the move), the RBNZ hike bias and 225bp NZD/CHF carry advantage support the bid. NZD/USD price action confirms the breakout targeting 0.5950. JPY remains under pressure ahead of Tokyo CPI, which acts as the next policy catalyst.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7844 — IV at 19.9% vs HV30 at 6.2% creates a massive +13.7pt VRP, signaling extreme institutional hedging demand for CHF protection. This institutional fear diverges from retail sentiment, where 76% of traders are long USD/CHF. The 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage favors USD, but the vol premium suggests the market is pricing a sharp safe-haven reversal. Watch for VIX > 25 to trigger carry unwind.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7159 — COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) shows crowded institutional positioning while retail is 72% short, creating a sharp institutional-retail divergence. IV at 14.2% vs HV30 at 8.2% (VRP +6.0%) indicates high cost for protection. The -60bp carry deficit vs USD makes the long AUD trade expensive to hold if US data continues to beat expectations (USD CESI +19). Watch the 16 Jun RBA meeting for policy support.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.35 — The 300bp Fed-BoJ carry spread sustains the pair's bid despite softer US PCE data and US-Iran peace hopes. 25d RR at -1.11 shows a moderate put skew as the options market pays for protection against BoJ intervention near 160.00. JPY CESI at +12 suggests domestic data is improving, but carry remains the dominant driver until Tokyo CPI or a break of 160.00.
